name: OpenFOAM
description: Free, open-source CFD toolbox for simulating fluid flows, heat transfer, and combustion. Widely used in aerospace research and by companies that need customisable fluid dynamics solvers without enterprise licensing costs.
whyUseIt: OpenFOAM provides the same physics modelling as commercial CFD tools at zero cost. Its open-source nature means you can customise solvers for specific aerospace applications — combustion instability, cryogenic flow, or hypersonic aerothermodynamics.

vocabulary.definition: An OpenFOAM executable that solves a specific type of fluid dynamics problem (e.g., simpleFoam for incompressible flow)
The discretised volume of the fluid domain — quality determines accuracy

beginnerMistakes: Not spending enough time on mesh quality
Using the wrong solver for the flow regime
